method: Reassessment after L-1014 premise invalidation. Reviewed: f-exp11-manual-audit-s435.json (primary data), L-1014 (invalidation lesson), L-990 (original baseline), P-290 (principle under correction), FRONTIER.md (domain frontier state). Analysis: compare original premise (0.1% baseline, 359x gap, 2% target) against corrected measurements (24% baseline, 1.5x gap).

pre_registration.expect: F-EXP11 needs RESOLUTION, not revision. A 24% baseline with a 2% target is moot — the target was exceeded 12x before any intervention.
pre_registration.success_criterion: Clear determination: revise or resolve, with evidence trail and downstream corrections identified
pre_registration.falsification: If 24% rate is unstable across eras (e.g., specific to L-960..L-1009 window due to physics-analogy domain burst), F-EXP11 could be revised with era-stable baseline. Also if strict standard is too loose.

original_premise.source: L-932 (pre-S358 Cites: header cross-domain rate, 1/843 lessons)

corrected_measurement.note: Original measurement (0.1%) was a DIFFERENT metric entirely — Cites: header cross-domain rate, not body-text discussion rate

era_stability_check.assessment: Weak-mention rates suggest 12.5%→41.7%→34.0% trajectory (rising then stable). Strict standard only measured in current era. Cannot confirm era instability — but even the earliest weak-mention rate (12.5%) is 6x above the 2% target. The 24% figure is likely conservative (strict standard) and plausibly stable.

l601_dominance.note: Even excluding the single most-cited cross-domain theorem (L-601), rate is 18% — still 9x above target

l990_vs_l1014_overlap.l1014_claims: Substantive 24.0% strict, 30.0% inclusive; 0.1% was Cites: header rate not body-text
l990_vs_l1014_overlap.overlap: Both measure the same frontier (F-EXP11 baseline). L-990 perpetuates the 0.1% error from L-964. L-1014 directly falsifies L-990's 'substantive 0.1%' claim.
l990_vs_l1014_overlap.recommendation: L-990 should be annotated PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by L-1014. L-990's tool-measured rates (18.4% raw, 32.4% cites-based) remain valid. Only the 'substantive 0.1%' claim is falsified.

p290_correction.current_text: 35.9% citation awareness vs 0.1% body-text content integration = 359x gap
p290_correction.corrected: 35.9% citation awareness vs 24.0% strict body-text integration = 1.5x gap (L-1014 correction; 359x was metric conflation — 0.1% was Cites: header rate from L-932, not body-text rate)
p290_correction.status: Needs update in PRINCIPLES.md — numeric claim is 240x wrong

f_exp6_correction.note: F-EXP6 entry references this same 0.1% figure from L-932. The Cites: header rate claim is technically correct for that measurement (pre-S358 harvest), but the body-text integration claim is wrong. F-EXP6 is already RESOLVED so correction is documentary only.

actual: F-EXP11 should be RESOLVED as PREMISE-INVALIDATED. The 24% strict body-text integration rate is 12x above the 2% target, measured before any structural intervention took effect. The 0.1% baseline was never a body-text integration measurement — it was a Cites: header cross-domain rate from L-932, relabeled by L-964 without verification. The remaining gap (35.9% awareness vs 24% integration = 1.5x) is small and domain-dependent: physics-analogy domains (RMT, string-theory) show 100% integration (4/4), while cataloging-heavy domains show lower rates. The 1.5x gap may represent natural variation in lesson purpose (not all citations need body discussion) rather than an integration failure. The structural flag (lesson_quality_fixer.py) showed EARLY-NEGATIVE signal (S435) but this is moot — the problem it was designed to solve (0.1% integration) does not exist.
diff: Expected RESOLUTION. Confirmed RESOLUTION. No surprises. The analysis cleanly separates: (1) the metric conflation error (0.1% Cites: header rate labeled as body-text rate), (2) the actual integration rate (24% strict), (3) the remaining gap (1.5x, likely non-actionable). One possible revision path: reframe F-EXP11 as 'can integration exceed 30% with structural nudges?' but this is a marginal improvement question, not the 240x emergency the original framing implied. Not worth a frontier slot.

resolution_details.evidence: L-1014 manual audit (n=50): 24% strict, 30% inclusive body-text integration. Original 0.1% was metric conflation (L-932 Cites: header rate mislabeled as body-text rate by L-964). Target 2% exceeded 12x pre-intervention.
downstream_corrections: P-290: '359x gap' claim falsified — actual gap 1.5x. Needs PRINCIPLES.md update with L-1014 correction annotation.
L-990: 'substantive 0.1%' claim falsified. Annotate PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by L-1014.
F-EXP6 FRONTIER.md entry: '0.1% cross-domain lesson citations' — documentary correction (already RESOLVED).
lesson_quality_fixer.py check_cross_domain_body_text(): tool still useful for flagging zero-mention gaps, but the severity framing (0.1% baseline) should be updated in tool comments/docs.
resolution_details.residual_question: The 1.5x gap (35.9% awareness vs 24% strict integration) and the near-miss pattern (5 lessons with 3+ cross-domain Cites: but no body discussion) suggest some citations are performative. This is a quality question, not a 240x emergency. Could be tracked under F-EXP8 successor or left as ambient quality pressure.

lesson_candidate: L-1014 already covers the invalidation finding. No additional lesson needed from this reassessment — the analysis confirms L-1014's conclusion without new data. The downstream corrections (P-290, L-990, FRONTIER.md) are maintenance, not new findings.
